1057→    private fun startNoIndentZone(node: ASTNode) =
1058→        IndentContext(
1059→            fromASTNode = node,
1060→            nodeIndent = "",
1061→            firstChildIndent = "",
1062→            childIndent = "",
1063→            lastChildIndent = "",
1064→            activated = true,
1065→        )
1066→
1067→    private companion object {
1068→        const val KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT = " "
1069→        const val TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT = "      " // Length of keyword "where" plus separating space
1070→    }
1071→
1072→    private data class IndentContext(
1073→        /**
1074→         * The node on which the indent context starts.
1075→         */
1076→        val fromASTNode: ASTNode,
1077→
1078→        /**
1079→         * The node at which the indent context ends. If null, then the context ends at the last child leaf of the node
1080→         * on which the indent context starts.
1081→         */
1082→        val toASTNode: ASTNode = fromASTNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
1083→
1084→        /**
1085→         * Cumulative indentation for the node. Normally this should be equal to a multiple of the
1086→         * 'indentConfig.indentStyle' to ensure a consistent indentation style.
1087→         */
1088→        val nodeIndent: String,
1089→
1090→        /**
1091→         * Additional indentation for first child node. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to
1092→         * ensure a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it
1093→         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1094→         */
1095→        val firstChildIndent: String,
1096→
1097→        /**
1098→         * Additional indentation for child nodes. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to ensure
1099→         * a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it is
1100→         * still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1101→         */
1102→        val childIndent: String,
1103→
1104→        /**
1105→         * Additional indentation for last child node. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to
1106→         * ensure a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it
1107→         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1108→         */
1109→        val lastChildIndent: String,
1110→
1111→        /**
1112→         * True when the indentation level of this context is activated
1113→         */
1114→        val activated: Boolean = false,
1115→    ) {
1116→        val nodes: String
1117→            get() =
1118→                fromASTNode
1119→                    .prevLeaf() // The 'fromAstNode' itself needs to be returned by '.leaves()' call as well
1120→                    ?.leaves()
1121→                    .orEmpty()
1122→                    .takeWhile {
1123→                        // The 'toAstNode' itself needs to be included as well
1124→                        it != toASTNode.nextLeaf()
1125→                    }
1126→                    .joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
1127→                    .textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()
1128→
1129→        fun indent() =
1130→            if (activated) {
1131→                nodeIndent + childIndent
1132→            } else {
1133→                nodeIndent
1134→            }
1135→
1136→        fun prevCodeLeaf() = fromASTNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
1137→    }
1138→}
1139→
1140→private fun ASTNode.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline() = text.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()
1141→
1142→private fun String.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline(): String {
1143→    val (prefix, suffix) = if (this.all { it.isWhitespace() }) {
1144→        Pair("[", "]")
1145→    } else {
1146→        Pair("", "")
1147→    }
1148→    return prefix
1149→        .plus(
1150→            this
1151→                .replace("\t", "\\t")
1152→                .replace("\n", "\\n"),
1153→        ).plus(suffix)
1154→}

1156→private class StringTemplateIndenter(private val indentConfig: IndentConfig) {
1157→    fun visitClosingQuotes(
1158→        expectedIndent: String,
1159→        node: ASTNode,
1160→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
1161→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
1162→    ) {
1163→        require(node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE)
1164→        node
1165→            .let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
1166→            .takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
1167→            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
1168→            ?.let { _ ->
1169→                if (node.containsMixedIndentationCharacters()) {
1170→                    // It can not be determined with certainty how mixed indentation characters should be interpreted.
1171→                    // The trimIndent function handles tabs and spaces equally (one tabs equals one space) while the user
1172→                    // might expect that the tab size in the indentation is more than one space.
1173→                    emit(
1174→                        node.startOffset,
1175→                        "Indentation of multiline string should not contain both tab(s) and space(s)",
1176→                        false,
1177→                    )
1178→                    return
1179→                }
1180→
1181→                val prefixLength = node.getCommonPrefixLength()
1182→                val correctedExpectedIndent =
1183→                    if (node.prevLeaf()?.text == "\n") {
1184→                        // In case the opening quotes are placed at the start of the line, then the closing quotes
1185→                        // should have no indent as well.
1186→                        ""
1187→                    } else {
1188→                        expectedIndent
1189→                    }
1190→                node.children()
1191→                    .forEach {
1192→                        if (it.prevLeaf()?.text == "\n" &&
1193→                            (
1194→                                it.isLiteralStringTemplateEntry() ||
1195→                                    it.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() ||
1196→                                    it.isClosingQuote()
1197→                                )
1198→                        ) {
1199→                            val (actualIndent, actualContent) =
1200→                                if (it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote()) {
1201→                                    it.text.splitIndentAt(it.text.length)
1202→                                } else if (it.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() && it.isFirstNonBlankElementOnLine()) {
1203→                                    it.getFirstElementOnSameLine().text.splitIndentAt(correctedExpectedIndent.length)
1204→                                } else {
1205→                                    it.text.splitIndentAt(prefixLength)
1206→                                }
1207→                            if (indentConfig.containsUnexpectedIndentChar(actualIndent)) {
1208→                                val offsetFirstWrongIndentChar =
1209→                                    indentConfig.indexOfFirstUnexpectedIndentChar(actualIndent)
1210→                                emit(
1211→                                    it.startOffset + offsetFirstWrongIndentChar,
1212→                                    "Unexpected '${indentConfig.unexpectedIndentCharDescription}' character(s) in margin of multiline " +
1213→                                        "string",
1214→                                    true,
1215→                                )
1216→                                if (autoCorrect) {
1217→                                    (it.firstChildNode as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
1218→                                        correctedExpectedIndent + actualContent,
1219→                                    )
1220→                                }
1221→                            } else if (actualIndent != correctedExpectedIndent && it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote()) {
1222→                                // It is a deliberate choice not to fix the indents inside the string literal except the line which only contains
1223→                                // the closing quotes.
1224→                                emit(
1225→                                    it.startOffset,
1226→                                    "Unexpected indent of multiline string closing quotes",
1227→                                    true,
1228→                                )
1229→                                if (autoCorrect) {
1230→                                    if (it.firstChildNode == null) {
1231→                                        (it as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(
1232→                                            LeafPsiElement(REGULAR_STRING_PART, correctedExpectedIndent),
1233→                                        )
1234→                                    } else {
1235→                                        (it.firstChildNode as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
1236→                                            correctedExpectedIndent + actualContent,
1237→                                        )
1238→                                    }
1239→                                }
1240→                            }
1241→                        }
1242→                    }
1243→            }
1244→    }
1245→
1246→    /**
1247→     * Get the length of the indent which is shared by all lines inside the string template except for the indent of
1248→     * the closing quotes.
1249→     */
1250→    private fun ASTNode.getCommonPrefixLength() =
1251→        children()
1252→            .filterNot { it.elementType == OPEN_QUOTE }
1253→            .filterNot { it.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE }
1254→            .filter { it.prevLeaf()?.text == "\n" }
1255→            .filterNot { it.text == "\n" }
1256→            .let { indents ->
1257→                val indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote = indents
1258→                    .filterNot { it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote() }
1259→                if (indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote.count() > 0) {
1260→                    indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote
1261→                } else {
1262→                    indents
1263→                }
1264→            }
1265→            .map { it.text.indentLength() }
1266→            .minOrNull()
1267→            ?: 0
1268→
1269→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimIndent() = isFollowedBy("trimIndent()")
1270→
1271→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimMargin() = isFollowedBy("trimMargin()")
1272→
1273→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedBy(callExpressionName: String) =
1274→        this.node.nextSibling { it.elementType != DOT }
1275→            .let { it?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION && it.text == callExpressionName }
1276→
1277→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isMultiLine(): Boolean {
1278→        for (child in node.children()) {
1279→            if (child.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) {
1280→                val v = child.text
1281→                if (v == "\n") {
1282→                    return true
1283→                }
1284→            }
1285→        }
1286→        return false
1287→    }
1288→
1289→    private fun ASTNode.containsMixedIndentationCharacters(): Boolean {
1290→        assert((this.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression).isMultiLine())
1291→        val nonBlankLines = this
1292→            .text
1293→            .split("\n")
1294→            .filterNot { it.startsWith("\"\"\"") }
1295→            .filterNot { it.endsWith("\"\"\"") }
1296→            .filterNot { it.isBlank() }
1297→        val prefixLength = nonBlankLines.minOfOrNull { it.indentLength() } ?: 0
1298→        val distinctIndentCharacters = nonBlankLines
1299→            .joinToString(separator = "") {
1300→                it.splitIndentAt(prefixLength).first
1301→            }
1302→            .toCharArray()
1303→            .distinct()
1304→            .count()
1305→        return distinctIndentCharacters > 1
1306→    }
1307→
1308→    private fun ASTNode.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote() =
1309→        elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE || (text.isBlank() && nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE)
1310→
1311→    private fun ASTNode.isLiteralStringTemplateEntry() = elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY && text != "\n"
1312→
1313→    private fun ASTNode.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() =
1314→        elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY || elementType == SHORT_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY
1315→
1316→    private fun ASTNode.isClosingQuote() = elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE
1317→
1318→    private fun String.indentLength() = indexOfFirst { !it.isWhitespace() }.let { if (it == -1) length else it }
1319→
1320→    /**
1321→     * Splits the string at the given index or at the first non-white space character before that index. The returned pair
1322→     * consists of the indentation and the second part contains the remainder. Note that the second part still can start
1323→     * with whitespace characters in case the original strings starts with more white space characters than the requested
1324→     * split index.
1325→     */
1326→    private fun String.splitIndentAt(index: Int): Pair<String, String> {
1327→        assert(index >= 0)
1328→        val firstNonWhitespaceIndex = indexOfFirst { !it.isWhitespace() }.let {
1329→            if (it == -1) {
1330→                this.length
1331→            } else {
1332→                it
1333→            }
1334→        }
1335→        val safeIndex = kotlin.math.min(firstNonWhitespaceIndex, index)
1336→        return Pair(
1337→            first = this.take(safeIndex),
1338→            second = this.substring(safeIndex),
1339→        )
1340→    }
1341→
1342→    private fun ASTNode.getFirstElementOnSameLine(): ASTNode {
1343→        val firstLeafOnLine = prevLeaf { it.text == "\n" }
1344→        return if (firstLeafOnLine == null) {
1345→            this
1346→        } else {
1347→            firstLeafOnLine.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true) ?: this
1348→        }
1349→    }
1350→
1351→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstNonBlankElementOnLine(): Boolean {
1352→        var node: ASTNode? = getFirstElementOnSameLine()
1353→        while (node != null && node != this && node.text.isWhitespace()) {
1354→            node = node.nextLeaf()
1355→        }
1356→        return node != this
1357→    }
1358→
1359→    private fun String.isWhitespace() = none { !it.isWhitespace() }
1360→}
1361→
1362→public val INDENTATION_RULE_ID: RuleId = IndentationRule().ruleId

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.internal.importordering
  2→
  3→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.resolve.ImportPath
  4→
  5→/**
  6→ * Represents an entry in the imports layout pattern. Contains matching logic for imports.
  7→ *
  8→ * Adopted from https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinPackageEntry.kt#L10
  9→ */
 10→public class PatternEntry(
 11→    packageName: String,
 12→    public val withSubpackages: Boolean,
 13→    public val hasAlias: Boolean,
 14→) {
 15→    private val packageName = packageName.removeSuffix(".*")
 16→
 17→    private fun matchesPackageName(otherPackageName: String): Boolean {
 18→        if (this == ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY || this == ALL_OTHER_ALIAS_IMPORTS_ENTRY) return true
 19→        if (this == BLANK_LINE_ENTRY) return false
 20→
 21→        if (otherPackageName.startsWith(packageName)) {
 22→            if (otherPackageName.length == packageName.length) {
 23→                return true
 24→            }
 25→            if (withSubpackages && otherPackageName[packageName.length] == '.') {
 26→                return true
 27→            }
 28→        }
 29→        return false
 30→    }
 31→
 32→    internal fun matches(import: ImportPath): Boolean {
 33→        return matchesPackageName(import.pathStr.removeSuffix(".*"))
 34→    }
 35→
 36→    internal fun isBetterMatchForPackageThan(
 37→        entry: PatternEntry?,
 38→        import: ImportPath,
 39→    ): Boolean {
 40→        if (hasAlias != import.hasAlias() || !matchesPackageName(import.pathStr)) return false
 41→        if (entry == null) return true
 42→
 43→        if (entry.hasAlias != hasAlias) return false
 44→        // Any matched package is better than ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY
 45→        if (this == ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY) return false
 46→        if (entry == ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY) return true
 47→
 48→        if (entry.withSubpackages != withSubpackages) return !withSubpackages
 49→
 50→        return entry.packageName.count { it == '.' } < packageName.count { it == '.' }
 51→    }
 52→
 53→    override fun toString(): String =
 54→        when (this) {
 55→            ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY -> WILDCARD_CHAR
 56→            ALL_OTHER_ALIAS_IMPORTS_ENTRY -> ALIAS_CHAR
 57→            else -> "$packageName.$WILDCARD_CHAR" + if (withSubpackages) WILDCARD_CHAR else ""
 58→        }
 59→
 60→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean {
 61→        if (this === other) return true
 62→        if (javaClass != other?.javaClass) return false
 63→
 64→        other as PatternEntry
 65→
 66→        if (withSubpackages != other.withSubpackages) return false
 67→        if (hasAlias != other.hasAlias) return false
 68→        if (packageName != other.packageName) return false
 69→
 70→        return true
 71→    }
 72→
 73→    override fun hashCode(): Int {
 74→        var result = withSubpackages.hashCode()
 75→        result = 31 * result + hasAlias.hashCode()
 76→        result = 31 * result + packageName.hashCode()
 77→        return result
 78→    }
 79→
 80→    internal companion object {
 81→        val BLANK_LINE_ENTRY: PatternEntry =
 82→            PatternEntry(
 83→                BLANK_LINE_CHAR,
 84→                withSubpackages = true,
 85→                hasAlias = false,
 86→            )
 87→
 88→        val ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY: PatternEntry =
 89→            PatternEntry(
 90→                WILDCARD_CHAR,
 91→                withSubpackages = true,
 92→                hasAlias = false,
 93→            )
 94→
 95→        val ALL_OTHER_ALIAS_IMPORTS_ENTRY: PatternEntry =
 96→            PatternEntry(
 97→                ALIAS_CHAR,
 98→                withSubpackages = true,
 99→                hasAlias = true,
100→            )
101→    }
102→}
103→

  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.NoWildcardImportsRule.Companion.IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.KtLintAssertThat.Companion.assertThatRule
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.LintViolation
  6→import org.junit.jupiter.api.Nested
  7→import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test
  8→
  9→class NoWildcardImportsRuleTest {
 10→    private val noWildcardImportsRuleAssertThat = assertThatRule { NoWildcardImportsRule() }
 11→
 12→    @Nested
 13→    inner class `Given that editorconfig property packagesToUseImportOnDemandProperty is not set` {
 14→        @Test
 15→        fun `Wildcard imports are detected`() {
 16→            val code =
 17→                """
 18→                import a
 19→                import a.b.*
 20→                import a.b.c
 21→                import a.d.*
 22→                import foo.bar.`**`
 23→                """.trimIndent()
 24→            noWildcardImportsRuleAssertThat(code)
 25→                .hasLintViolationsWithoutAutoCorrect(
 26→                    LintViolation(2, 1, "Wildcard import"),
 27→                    LintViolation(4, 1, "Wildcard import"),
 28→                )
 29→        }
 30→
 31→        @Test
 32→        fun `Wildcard imports on packages which are accepted by IntelliJ Default are not detected`() {
 33→            val code =
 34→                """
 35→                import a.b
 36→                import kotlinx.android.synthetic.main.layout_name.*
 37→                """.trimIndent()
 38→            noWildcardImportsRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
 39→        }
 40→    }
 41→
 42→    @Nested
 43→    inner class `Given that editorconfig property packagesToUseImportOnDemandProperty is set` {
 44→        @Test
 45→        fun `Given that the property is set with value 'unset' then packages which are accepted by IntelliJ Default are not detected`() {
 46→            val code =
 47→                """
 48→                import a.b
 49→                import kotlinx.android.synthetic.main.layout_name.*
 50→                import react.*
 51→                import react.dom.*
 52→                """.trimIndent()
 53→            noWildcardImportsRuleAssertThat(code)
 54→                .withEditorConfigOverride(IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND to "unset")
 55→                .hasLintViolationsWithoutAutoCorrect(
 56→                    LintViolation(3, 1, "Wildcard import"),
 57→                    LintViolation(4, 1, "Wildcard import"),
 58→                )
 59→        }
 60→
 61→        @Test
 62→        fun `Given that the property is set to some packages exclusive subpackages then wildcard imports for those directories are not detected`() {
 63→            val code =
 64→                """
 65→                import a.b
 66→                import kotlinx.android.synthetic.main.layout_name.*
 67→                import react.*
 68→                import react.dom.*
 69→                """.trimIndent()
 70→            noWildcardImportsRuleAssertThat(code)
 71→                .withEditorConfigOverride(IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND to "react.*,react.dom.*")
 72→                .hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(2, 1, "Wildcard import")
 73→        }
 74→
 75→        @Test
 76→        fun `Given that the property is set to some packages inclusive subpackages then wildcard imports for those directories are not detected`() {
 77→            val code =
 78→                """
 79→                import a.b
 80→                import kotlinx.android.synthetic.main.layout_name.*
 81→                import react.*
 82→                import react.dom.*
 83→                """.trimIndent()
 84→            noWildcardImportsRuleAssertThat(code)
 85→                .withEditorConfigOverride(IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND to "react.**")
 86→                .hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(2, 1, "Wildcard import")
 87→        }
 88→
 89→        @Test
 90→        fun `Given that property is set without a value then the packages which otherwise would be accepted by IntelliJ Default are detected`() {
 91→            val code =
 92→                """
 93→                import a.b
 94→                import kotlinx.android.synthetic.main.layout_name.*
 95→                """.trimIndent()
 96→            noWildcardImportsRuleAssertThat(code)
 97→                .withEditorConfigOverride(IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND to "")
 98→                .hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(2, 1, "Wildcard import")
 99→        }
100→    }
101→}

1→## Annotation formatting
  2→
  3→Multiple annotations should be on a separate line than the annotated declaration; annotations with parameters should each be on separate lines; annotations should be followed by a space
  4→
  5→Rule-id: `annotation`
  6→
  7→## Argument list wrapping
  8→
  9→Rule-id: `argument-list-wrapping`
 10→
 11→## Chain wrapping
 12→
 13→When wrapping chained calls `.`, `?.` and `?:` should be placed on the next line
 14→
 15→Rule id: `chain-wrapping`
 16→
 17→## Enum entry
 18→
 19→Enum entry names should be uppercase underscore-separated names.
 20→
 21→Rule id: `enum-entry-name-case`
 22→
 23→## File name
 24→
 25→Files containing only one toplevel domain should be named according to that element.
 26→
 27→Rule id: `filename`
 28→
 29→## Final newline
 30→
 31→Ensures consistent usage of a newline at the end of each file. 
 32→
 33→This rule can be configured with `.editorconfig` property [`insert_final_newline`](../configuration-ktlint/#final-newline).
 34→
 35→Rule id: `final-newline`
 36→
 37→## Import ordering
 38→
 39→Imports ordered consistently (see [Custom ktlint EditorConfig properties](#custom-ktlint-specific-editorconfig-properties) for more)
 40→
 41→Rule id: `import-ordering`
 42→
 43→## Indentation
 44→
 45→Indentation formatting - respects `.editorconfig` `indent_size` with no continuation indent (see [EditorConfig](../configuration-ktlint/) section for more).
 46→
 47→Rule id: `indent`
 48→
 49→## Max line length
 50→
 51→Ensures that lines do not exceed the given length of `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` (see [EditorConfig](../configuration-ktlint/) section for more). This rule does not apply in a number of situations. For example, in the case a line exceeds the maximum line length due to and comment that disables ktlint rules than that comment is being ignored when validating the length of the line. The `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_ignore_back_ticked_identifier` can be set to ignore identifiers which are enclosed in backticks, which for example is very useful when you want to allow longer names for unit tests.
 52→
 53→Rule id: `max-line-length`
 54→
 55→## Modifier order
 56→
 57→Consistent order of modifiers
 58→
 59→Rule id: `modifier-order`
 60→
 61→## Multiline if-else
 62→
 63→Braces required for multiline if/else statements.
 64→
 65→Rule id: `multiline-if-else`
 66→
 67→## No blank lines before `}`
 68→
 69→No blank lines before `}`.
 70→
 71→Rule id: `no-blank-line-before-rbrace`
 72→
 73→## No blank lines in chained method calls
 74→
 75→Rule id: `no-blank-lines-in-chained-method-calls`
 76→
 77→## No consecutive blank lines
 78→ 
 79→Rule id: `no-consecutive-blank-lines`
 80→
 81→## No empty (`{}`) class bodies
 82→
 83→Rule id: `no-empty-class-body`
 84→
 85→## No leading empty lines in method blocks
 86→ 
 87→Rule id: `no-empty-first-line-in-method-block`
 88→
 89→## No line break after else
 90→
 91→Disallows line breaks after the else keyword if that could lead to confusion, for example:
 92→```kotlin
 93→if (conditionA()) {
 94→    doSomething()
 95→} else
 96→if (conditionB()) {
 97→    doAnotherThing()
 98→}
 99→```
100→
101→Rule id: `no-line-break-after-else`
102→
103→## No line break before assignment 
104→
105→When a line is broken at an assignment (`=`) operator the break comes after the symbol.
106→
107→Rule id: `no-line-break-before-assignment`
108→
109→## No multi spaces
110→
111→Except in indentation and in KDoc's it is not allowed to have multiple consecutive spaces.
112→
113→Rule id: `no-multi-spaces`
114→
115→## No semicolons
116→
117→No semicolons (unless used to separate multiple statements on the same line).
118→
119→Rule id: `no-semi`
120→
121→## No trailing whitespaces
122→
123→Rule id: `no-trailing-spaces`

134→    ```

139→    ```
140→
141→Rule id: `no-unit-return`
142→
143→## No unused imports
144→
145→Rule id: `no-unused-imports`
146→
147→## No wildcard imports
148→

150→
151→!!! warning
152→
153→    In case property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand` is not explicitly set, it allows wildcards imports like `java.util.*` by default to keep in sync with IntelliJ IDEA behavior. To disallow *all* wildcard imports, add property below to your `.editorconfig`:

157→    ```
158→
159→Rule id: `no-wildcard-imports`

176→

185→## String template
186→

188→
189→Rule id: `string-template`

191→## Trailing comma on call site
192→
193→Consistent removal (default) or adding of trailing comma's on call site.
194→
195→!!! important
196→    KtLint uses the IntelliJ IDEA `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site` to configure the rule. When this property is enabled, KtLint *enforces* the usage of the trailing comma at call site while IntelliJ IDEA default formatter only *allows* to use the trailing comma but leaves it to the developer's discretion to actually use it (or not). KtLint values *consistent* formatting more than a per-situation decision.
197→
198→!!! note
199→    In KtLint 0.48.x the default value for using the trailing comma on call site has been changed to `true` except when codestyle `android` is used.
200→    
201→    Although the [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#names-for-test-methods) leaves it to the developer's discretion to use trailing comma's on the call site, it also states that usage of trailing commas has several benefits:
202→    
203→     * It makes version-control diffs cleaner – as all the focus is on the changed value.
204→     * It makes it easy to add and reorder elements – there is no need to add or delete the comma if you manipulate elements.
205→     * It simplifies code generation, for example, for object initializers. The last element can also have a comma.
206→
207→!!! note
208→    Trailing comma on call site is automatically disabled if the [Wrapping](#wrapping) rule (or, before version `0.45.0`, the [Indentation](#indentation) rule) is disabled or not loaded. Because it cannot provide proper formatting with unwrapped calls. (see [dependencies](./dependencies.md)).
209→
210→    === "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
211→
212→        ```kotlin
213→        FooWrapper(
214→            Foo(
215→                a = 3,
216→                b = 4,
217→            ),
218→        )
219→        ```
220→    === "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
221→
222→        ```kotlin
223→        FooWrapper(Foo(
224→            a = 3,
225→            b = 4,
226→        ),) // it's weird to insert "," between unwrapped (continued) parenthesis
227→        ```
228→
229→
230→Rule id: `trailing-comma-on-call-site`
231→
232→## Trailing comma on declaration site
233→
234→Consistent removal (default) or adding of trailing comma's on declaration site.
235→
236→!!! important
237→    KtLint uses the IntelliJ IDEA `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma` to configure the rule. When this property is enabled, KtLint *enforces* the usage of the trailing comma at declaration site while IntelliJ IDEA default formatter only *allows* to use the trailing comma but leaves it to the developer's discretion to actually use it (or not). KtLint values *consistent* formatting more than a per-situation decision.
238→
239→!!! note
240→    In KtLint 0.48.x the default value for using the trailing comma on declaration site has been changed to `true` except when codestyle `android` is used.
241→
242→    The [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#names-for-test-methods) encourages the usage of trailing comma's on the declaration site, but leaves it to the developer's discretion to use trailing comma's on the call site. But next to this, it also states that usage of trailing commas has several benefits:
243→    
244→     * It makes version-control diffs cleaner – as all the focus is on the changed value.
245→     * It makes it easy to add and reorder elements – there is no need to add or delete the comma if you manipulate elements.
246→     * It simplifies code generation, for example, for object initializers. The last element can also have a comma.
247→
248→!!! note
249→    Trailing comma on declaration site is automatically disabled if the [Wrapping](#wrapping) rule (or, before version `0.45.0`, the [Indentation](#indentation) rule) is disabled or not loaded. Because it cannot provide proper formatting with unwrapped declarations. (see [dependencies](./dependencies.md)).
250→
251→    === "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
252→
253→        ```kotlin
254→        class FooWrapper(
255→            val foo = Foo(
256→                a = 3,
257→                b = 4,
258→            ),
259→        )
260→        ```
261→    === "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
262→
263→        ```kotlin
264→        class FooWrapper(val foo = Foo(
265→            a = 3,
266→            b = 4,
267→        ),) // it's weird to insert "," between unwrapped (continued) parenthesis
268→        ```
269→
270→
271→Rule id: `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site`
272→

324→
325→Consistent spacing around keywords.
326→
327→Rule id: `keyword-spacing`
328→
329→### Operator spacing
330→
331→Consistent spacing around operators.
332→
333→Rule id: `op-spacing`
334→
335→### Parenthesis spacing
336→
337→Consistent spacing around parenthesis.
338→
339→Rule id: `paren-spacing`
340→
341→### Range spacing
342→
343→Consistent spacing around range operators.
344→
345→Rule id: `range-spacing`
346→
347→### Angle bracket spacing
348→
349→No spaces around angle brackets when used for typing.
350→
351→Rule id: `spacing-around-angle-brackets`
352→
353→### Blank line between declarations with annotations
354→
355→Declarations with annotations should be separated by a blank line.
356→
357→Rule id: `spacing-between-declarations-with-annotations`
358→
359→### Blank line between declaration with comments
360→
361→Declarations with comments should be separated by a blank line.
362→
363→Rule id: `spacing-between-declarations-with-comments`
364→
365→### Unary operator spacing
366→
367→No spaces around unary operators.
368→
369→Rule id: `unary-op-spacing`

1→## Why should I use ktlint?
  2→
  3→the short answer is **Simplicity**.
  4→
  5→Spending time on configuration (and maintenance down the road) of hundred-line long style config file(s) is counter-productive. Instead of wasting your energy on something that has no business value - focus on what really matters (not debating whether to use tabs or spaces).
  6→
  7→By using ktlint you put the importance of code clarity and community conventions over personal preferences. This makes things easier for people reading your code as well as frees you from having to document and explain what style potential contributor(s) have to follow.
  8→
  9→ktlint is a single binary with both linter & formatter included. All you need is to drop it in (no need to get [overwhelmed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Decision_fatigue) while choosing among [dozens of code style options](https://checkstyle.sourceforge.net/checks.html)).
 10→
 11→## Can I have my own rules on top of ktlint?
 12→
 13→Absolutely, "no configuration" doesn't mean "no extensibility". You can add your own ruleset(s) to discover potential bugs, check for anti-patterns, etc.
 14→
 15→See [adding a custom rule set](../api/custom-rule-set/) for more information.
 16→
 17→## How do I suppress errors for a line/block/file?
 18→
 19→!!! tip
 20→    Suppressing a `ktlint` violation is meant primarily as an escape latch for the rare cases when **ktlint** is not able to produce the correct result. Please report any such instances using [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues)).
 21→
 22→To disable a specific rule you'll need the rule identifier which is displayed at the end of the lint error.
 23→
 24→An error can be suppressed using:
 25→
 26→* EOL comments
 27→* Block comments
 28→* @Suppress annotations
 29→
 30→From a consistency perspective seen, it might be best to **not** mix the (EOL/Block) comment style with the annotation style in the same project.
 31→
 32→### Disabling for one specific line using EOL comment
 33→
 34→An error for a specific rule on a specific line can be disabled with an EOL comment on that line:
 35→
 36→```kotlin
 37→import foo.* // ktlint-disable no-wildcard-imports
 38→```
 39→
 40→In case lint errors for different rules on the same line need to be ignored, then specify multiple rule ids (separated by a space):
 41→
 42→```kotlin
 43→import foo.* // ktlint-disable no-wildcard-imports other-rule-id
 44→```
 45→
 46→In case all lint errors on a line need to be ignored, then do not specify the rule id at all:
 47→
 48→```kotlin
 49→import foo.* // ktlint-disable
 50→```
 51→
 52→### Disabling for a block of lines using Block comments
 53→
 54→An error for a specific rule in a block of lines can be disabled with an block comment like:
 55→
 56→```kotlin
 57→/* ktlint-disable no-wildcard-imports */
 58→import package.a.*
 59→import package.b.*
 60→/* ktlint-enable no-wildcard-imports */
 61→```
 62→
 63→In case lint errors for different rules in the same block of lines need to be ignored, then specify multiple rule ids (separated by a space):
 64→
 65→```kotlin
 66→/* ktlint-disable no-wildcard-imports other-rule-id */
 67→import package.a.*
 68→import package.b.*
 69→/* ktlint-enable no-wildcard-imports,other-rule-id */
 70→```
 71→
 72→Note that the `ktlint-enable` directive needs to specify the exact same rule-id's and in the same order as the `ktlint-disable` directive.
 73→
 74→In case all lint errors in a block of lines needs to be ignored, then do not specify the rule id at all:
 75→
 76→```kotlin
 77→/* ktlint-disable */
 78→import package.a.*
 79→import package.b.*
 80→/* ktlint-enable */
 81→```
 82→
 83→### Disabling for a statement or an entire file using @Suppress
 84→
 85→!!! tip
 86→    As of ktlint version 0.46, it is possible to specify any ktlint rule id via the `@Suppress` annotation in order to suppress errors found by that rule. Note that some rules like `indent` still do not support disabling for parts of a file.
 87→
 88→An error for a specific rule on a specific line can be disabled with a `@Suppress` annotation:
 89→
 90→```kotlin
 91→@Suppress("ktlint:max-line-length", "ktlint:some-custom-rule-set-id:some-custom-rule-id")
 92→val foo = listOf(
 93→    "some really looooooooooooooooong string exceeding the max line length",
 94→  )
 95→```
 96→
 97→Note that when using `@Suppress` each rule id needs to be prefixed with `ktlint:` in case of standard rules provided by ktlint and with `ktlint:some-custom-rule-set-id:` in case the rule is provided by the custom rule set with id `some-custom-rule-set-id`. 
 98→
 99→To suppress the violations of all ktlint rules, use:
100→```kotlin
101→@Suppress("ktlint")
102→val foo = "some really looooooooooooooooong string exceeding the max line length"
103→```
104→
105→Like with other `@Suppress` annotations, it can be placed on targets supported by the annotation. As of this it is possible to disable rules in the entire file with:
106→```kotlin
107→@file:Suppress("ktlint") // Suppressing all rules for the entire file
108→```
109→or
110→```
111→@file:Suppress("ktlint:max-line-length", "ktlint:some-custom-rule-set-id:some-custom-rule-id") // Suppressing specific rules for the entire file
112→```
113→
114→## How do I globally disable a rule?
115→With [`.editorConfig` property `disabled_rules`](../rules/configuration-ktlint#disabled-rules) a rule can be disabled globally.
116→
117→You may also pass a list of disabled rules via the `--disabled_rules` command line flag. It has the same syntax as the EditorConfig property.
118→
119→
120→## Why is `.editorconfig` property `disabled_rules` deprecated and how do I resolve this?
121→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disbaled_rules` are deprecated as of KtLint version `0.48` and are marked for removal in version `0.49`. Those properties contain a comma separated list of rules which are disabled. Using a comma separated list of values has some disadvantages.
122→
123→A big disadvantage is that it is not possible to override the property partially in an `.editorconfig` file in a subpackage. Another disadvantage is that it is not possible to express explicitly that a rule is enabled. Lastly, (qualified) rule ids can be 20 characters or longer, which makes a list with multiple entries hard to read.
124→
125→*Root `.editorconfig`*
126→```editorconfig
127→root = true
128→
129→[*.kt]
130→disabled_rules=rule-1,rule-2,rule-3
131→```
132→This `.editorconfig` defines that all rules except `rule-1`, `rule-2` and `rule-3` should be run in all packages. Suppose that we want to enable `rule-1` but disable `rule-4` in certain subpackage, then we would need to define an `.editorconfig` file like below:
133→
134→*Secondary `.editorconfig`*
135→```editorconfig
136→[*.kt]
137→disabled_rules=rule-2,rule-4,rule-3
138→```
139→Disabling another rule in the root `.editorconfig` file, does not have effect on this subpackage as long as that rule has not been added to the `.editorconfig` file in the subpackage. 
140→
141→Starting with KtLint `0.48` entire rule sets and individual rules can be disabled / enabled with a separate property per rule (set). 
142→
143→All rules in a rule set can be enabled or disabled with a rule set property. The name of the rule set property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id. Examples:
144→```editorconfig
145→ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
146→ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable rules marked as experimental for all rule sets that are enabled
147→ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
148→```
149→
150→!!! note
151→    All rules from the `standard` and custom rule sets are *enabled* by default and can optionally be disabled in the `.editorconfig`. All rules from the `experimental` rule set are *disabled* by default and can optionally be enabled in the `.editorconfig`.
152→
153→An individual property can be enabled or disabled with a rule property. The name of the rule property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id followed by a `_` and the rule id. Examples:
154→```editorconfig
155→ktlint_standard_final-newline = disabled # Disables the `final-newline` rule in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
156→ktlint_standard_some-experimental-rule = enabled # Enables the (experimental) `some-experimental-rule` in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
157→ktlint_custom-rule-set_custom-rule = disabled # Disables the `custom-rule` rule in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
158→```
159→
160→!!! note
161→    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.  
162→
163→## Why is wildcard import `java.util.*` not reported by the `no-wildcard-imports` rule?
164→
165→The `no-wildcard-imports` rule forbids wildcard imports, except for imports defined in `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand`. If ths property is not set explicitly set, it allows wildcards imports like `java.util.*` by default to keep in sync with IntelliJ IDEA behavior.
166→

1→Ktlint uses a limited set of `.editorconfig` properties for additional configuration. A sensible default value is provided for each property when not explicitly defined. Properties can be overridden, provided they are specified under `[*.{kt,kts}]`. Ktlint uses some properties defined by [.editorconfig](https://editorconfig.org/), IntelliJ IDEA and custom properties.
  2→
  3→!!! danger
  4→
  5→    Unfortunately [IntelliJ IDEA](https://www.jetbrains.com/idea/) has an [autoformat issue regarding `.editorconfig`](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/IDEA-242506). Due to this error an additional space is added between glob statements, resulting in `[*{kt, kts}]` instead of `[*{kt,kts}]`. The `.editorconfig` library used by `ktlint` [ignores sections after encountering a space in the list](https://github.com/editorconfig/editorconfig/issues/148). As a result, the rule is not applied on all files as documented in the [original ktlint issue](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/762).
  6→
  7→## Code style
  8→
  9→By default, the `offical` Kotlin code style is applied. Alternatively, the code style can be set to `android`.
 10→
 11→```ini
 12→[*.{kt,kts}]
 13→ktlint_code_style = official
 14→```
 15→
 16→## Disabled rules
 17→
 18→!!! note
 19→    Support of properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` has been removed in KtLint `0.49`.
 20→
 21→Rule sets and individual rules can be disabled / enabled with a separate property per rule (set).
 22→
 23→All rules in a rule set can be enabled or disabled with a rule set property. The name of the rule set property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id. Examples:
 24→```editorconfig
 25→ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 26→ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable all rules from the `experimental` rule set provided by KtLint
 27→ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
 28→```
 29→
 30→Rules that are marked as experimental will not be run, unless explicitly enabled:
 31→```editorconfig
 32→ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable rules marked as experimental for all rule sets that are enabled
 33→```
 34→
 35→An individual rule can be enabled or disabled with a rule property. The name of the rule property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id followed by a `_` and the rule id. Examples:
 36→```editorconfig
 37→ktlint_standard_final-newline = disabled # Disables the `final-newline` rule provided by KtLint
 38→ktlint_standard_some-experimental-rule = enabled # Enables the (experimental) `some-experimental-rule` in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 39→ktlint_custom-rule-set_custom-rule = disabled # Disables the `custom-rule` rule in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
 40→```
 41→
 42→!!! note
 43→    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.
 44→
 45→
 46→## Final newline
 47→
 48→By default, a final newline is required at the end of the file.
 49→
 50→```ini
 51→[*.{kt,kts}]
 52→insert_final_newline = true
 53→```
 54→
 55→This setting only takes effect when rule `final-newline` is enabled.
 56→
 57→## Force multiline function signature based on number of parameters
 58→
 59→By default, the number of parameters in a function signature is not relevant when rewriting the function signature. Only the maximum line length determines when a function signature should be written on a single line or with multiple lines. Setting `ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than` can be used, to force a multiline function signature in case the function contain at least a number of parameters even in case the function signature would fit on a single line. Use value `-1` (default) to disable this setting.
 60→
 61→```ini
 62→[*.{kt,kts}]
 63→ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than= -1
 64→```
 65→
 66→This setting only takes effect when rule `function-signature` is enabled.
 67→
 68→## Wrapping the expression body of a function
 69→
 70→Setting `ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping` determines if and when the expression body of a function is wrapped to a new line. This setting can be set to value `default`, `multiline` or `always`. 
 71→
 72→When set to `default`, the first line of a body expression is appended to the function signature as long as the max line length is not exceeded.
 73→
 74→```kotlin title="ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping=default (or when not set)"
 75→// Given that the function signature has to be written as a single line function signature
 76→fun someFunction(a: Any, b: Any): String = "some-result"
 77→    .uppercase()
 78→
 79→// Given that the function signature has to be written as a multiline function signature
 80→fun someFunction(
 81→    a: Any,
 82→    b: Any
 83→): String = "some-result"
 84→    .uppercase()
 85→```
 86→
 87→When set to `multiline`, the body expression starts on a separate line in case it is a multiline expression. A single line body expression is wrapped only when it does not fit on the same line as the function signature.
 88→
 89→```kotlin title="ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping=multiline"
 90→// Given a single line body expression and
 91→// a the function signature that has to be written as a single line function signature and
 92→// it does not exceed the max line length
 93→fun someFunction(a: Any, b: Any): String = "some-result".uppercase()
 94→
 95→// Given a single line body expression and
 96→// a the function signature that has to be written as a multiline function signature and
 97→// it does not exceed the max line length
 98→fun someFunction(
 99→    a: Any,
100→    b: Any
101→): String = "some-result".uppercase()
102→
103→// Given a single line body expression then always wrap it to a separate line
104→fun someFunction(a: Any, b: Any): String =
105→    "some-result"
106→         .uppercase()
107→fun someFunction(
108→    a: Any,
109→    b: Any
110→): String =
111→    "some-result"
112→       .uppercase()
113→```
114→
115→When set to `always` the body expression is always wrapped to a separate line.
116→
117→```kotlin title="ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping=always"
118→fun someFunction(a: Any, b: Any): String =
119→    "some-result".uppercase()
120→fun functionWithAVeryLongName(
121→    a: Any,
122→    b: Any
123→): String =
124→    "some-result"
125→        .uppercase()
126→```
127→
128→This setting only takes effect when rule `function-signature` is enabled.
129→
130→## Ignore identifiers enclosed in backticks
131→
132→By default, the identifiers enclosed in backticks are not ignored.
133→
134→According to [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#names-for-test-methods) it is acceptable to write method names in natural language. When using natural language, the description tends to be longer. This property allows lines containing an identifier between backticks to be longer than the maximum line length. (Since 0.41.0)
135→
136→```kotlin
137→@Test
138→fun `Given a test with a very loooooooooooooooooooooong test description`() {
139→    
140→}
141→```
142→
143→```ini
144→[*.{kt,kts}]
145→ktlint_ignore_back_ticked_identifier = false
146→```
147→
148→This setting only takes effect when rule `max-line-length` is enabled.
149→
150→## Import layouts
151→
152→By default, the same imports are allowed as in IntelliJ IDEA. The import path can be a full path, e.g. "java.util.List.*" as well as wildcard path, e.g. "kotlin.**".
153→
154→The layout can be composed by the following symbols:
155→
156→*  `*` - wildcard. There must be at least one entry of a single wildcard to match all other imports. Matches anything after a specified symbol/import as well.
157→* `|` - blank line. Supports only single blank lines between imports. No blank line is allowed in the beginning or end of the layout.
158→* `^` - alias import, e.g. "^android.*" will match all android alias imports, "^" will match all other alias imports.
159→
160→Examples:
161→```kotlin
162→ij_kotlin_imports_layout=* # alphabetical with capital letters before lower case letters (e.g. Z before a), no blank lines
163→ij_kotlin_imports_layout=*,java.**,javax.**,kotlin.**,^ # default IntelliJ IDEA style, same as alphabetical, but with "java", "javax", "kotlin" and alias imports in the end of the imports list
164→ij_kotlin_imports_layout=android.**,|,^org.junit.**,kotlin.io.Closeable.*,|,*,^ # custom imports layout
165→```
166→
167→Wildcard imports can be allowed for specific import paths (Comma-separated list, use "**" as wildcard for package and all subpackages). This setting overrides the no-wildcard-imports rule. This setting is best be used for allowing wildcard imports from libraries like Ktor where extension functions are used in a way that creates a lot of imports.
168→
169→```ini
170→[*.{kt,kts}]
171→ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand = java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**
172→```
173→
174→This setting only takes effect when rule `no-wildcard-imports` is enabled.
175→
176→## Indent size & style
177→
178→By default, indenting is done with 4 spaces per indent level in `official` Kotlin code style while a single tab is used by default in the `android` code style.
179→
180→```ini
181→[*.{kt,kts}]
182→indent_size = 4 # possible values: number (e.g. 2), "unset" (makes ktlint ignore indentation completely)  
183→indent_style = space # or "tab"
184→```
185→
186→Those settings are used by multiple rules of which rule `indent` is the most important.
187→
188→## Max line length
189→
190→By default, the maximum line length is not set. The `android` code style sets the max line length to 100 (per Android Kotlin Style Guide).
191→
192→```ini
193→[*.{kt,kts}]
194→max_line_length = off # Use "off" to ignore max line length or a positive number to set max line length
195→```
196→
197→This setting is used by multiple rules of which rule `max-line-length` is the most important.
198→
199→## Trailing comma on call site
200→
201→KtLint uses the IntelliJ IDEA `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site` to *enforce* the usage of the trailing comma at call site when enabled. IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to *allow* the use of trailing comma but leaves it to the developer's discretion to actually use it (or not). KtLint values *consistent* formatting more than a per-situation decision.
202→
203→!!! note
204→    In KtLint 0.48.x the default value for using the trailing comma on call site has been changed to `true` except when codestyle `android` is used.
205→
206→    Although the [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#names-for-test-methods) leaves it to the developer's discretion to use trailing comma's on the call site, it also states that usage of trailing commas has several benefits:
207→    
208→     * It makes version-control diffs cleaner – as all the focus is on the changed value.
209→     * It makes it easy to add and reorder elements – there is no need to add or delete the comma if you manipulate elements.
210→     * It simplifies code generation, for example, for object initializers. The last element can also have a comma.
211→
212→Example:
213→```ini
214→[*.{kt,kts}]
215→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site = false
216→```
217→
218→This setting only takes effect when rule `trailing-comma-on-call-site` is enabled.
219→
220→## Trailing comma on declaration site
221→
222→KtLint uses the IntelliJ IDEA `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma` to *enforce* the usage of the trailing comma at declaration site when enabled. IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to *allow* the use of trailing comma but leaves it to the developer's discretion to actually use it (or not). KtLint values *consistent* formatting more than a per-situation decision.
223→
224→!!! note
225→    In KtLint 0.48.x the default value for using the trailing comma on declaration site has been changed to `true` except when codestyle `android` is used.
226→
227→    The [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#names-for-test-methods) encourages the usage of trailing comma's on the declaration site, but leaves it to the developer's discretion to use trailing comma's on the call site. But next to this, it also states that usage of trailing commas has several benefits:
228→    
229→     * It makes version-control diffs cleaner – as all the focus is on the changed value.
230→     * It makes it easy to add and reorder elements – there is no need to add or delete the comma if you manipulate elements.
231→     * It simplifies code generation, for example, for object initializers. The last element can also have a comma.
232→
233→Example:
234→```ini
235→[*.{kt,kts}]
236→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma = false # Only used for declaration site 
237→```
238→
239→This setting only takes effect when rule `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` is enabled.
240→
241→## Overriding Editorconfig properties for specific directories
242→
243→You can [override](https://editorconfig.org/#file-format-details) properties for specific directories inside your project:
244→```ini
245→[*.{kt,kts}]
246→ktlint_standard_import-ordering = disabled
247→
248→[api/*.{kt,kts}]
249→ktlint_standard_indent = disabled
250→```
251→
252→Note that the `import-ordering` rule is disabled for *all* packages including the `api` sub package. Next to this the `indent` rule is disabled for the `api` package and its sub packages.

1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
  8→
  9→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 10→- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
 11→- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 12→- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 13→
 14→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 15→
 16→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 17→
 18→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 19→* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
 20→* KtLint disable and enable directives.
 21→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 22→
 23→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 24→
 25→#### Experimental rules
 26→
 27→Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
 28→
 29→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 30→
 31→#### EditorConfig
 32→
 33→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 34→
 35→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 36→
 37→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 38→
 39→A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
 40→
 41→The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
 42→
 43→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
 44→
 45→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 46→
 47→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 48→
 49→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 50→
 51→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.

151→#### Custom Ruleset Provider `RuleSetProviderV2`
152→
153→Custom Rule Sets build for older versions of KtLint no longer supported by this version of KtLint. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleSetProviderV2` interface has been replaced with `RuleSetProviderV3`. The accompanying interfaces `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleProvider` and `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule` have been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.RuleProvider` and `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule` respectively.
154→
155→Contrary to `RuleSetProviderV2`, the `RuleSetProviderV3` no longer contains information about the rule set. About information now has to be specified in the new `Rule` class. This allows custom rule set providers to combine rules originating from different rule sets into a new rule set without loosing information about its origin. The typ of the id of the rule set is changed from `String` to `RuleSetId`. 
156→
157→Note that due to renaming and relocation of the `RuleSetProviderV3` interface the name of the service provider in the custom reporter needs to be changed from `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleSetProviderV2` to `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.ruleset.core.api.RuleSetProviderV3`.
158→
159→The rule id's in `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule` have been changed from type `String` to `RuleId`. A `RuleId` has a value that must adhere the convention "`rule-set-id`:`rule-id`". The rule set id `standard` is reserved for rules which are maintained by the KtLint project. Rules created by custom rule set providers and API Consumers should use a prefix other than `standard` to mark the origin of rules which are not maintained by the KtLint project.
160→
161→When wrapping a rule from the ktlint project and modifying its behavior, please change the `ruleId` and `about` fields in the wrapped rule, so that it is clear to users whenever they use the original rule provided by KtLint versus a modified version which is not maintained by the KtLint project.
162→
163→The typealias `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig`. The interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties` has been removed. Instead, the Rule property `usesEditorConfigProperties` needs to be set. As a result of those changes, the `beforeFirstNode` function in each rule has to changed to something like below: 
164→
165→```kotlin
166→public class SomeRule : Rule(
167→  ruleId = RuleId("some-rule-set:some-rule"),
168→  usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY),
169→) {
170→  private lateinit var myEditorConfigProperty: MyEditorConfigProperty
171→
172→  override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
173→    myEditorConfigProperty = editorConfig[MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY]
174→  }
175→  
176→  ...
177→}
178→```
179→
180→Fields `loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded` and `runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled` have been removed from class `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule` and are replaced with a single field `mode`. The `mode` either contains value `REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED` or `ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED`.
181→
182→#### Custom Reporter Provider `ReporterProvider`
183→
184→Custom Reporters build for older versions of KtLint are no longer supported by this version of KtLint. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ReporterProvider` interface has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterProviderV2`. The accompanying interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Reporter` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterV2`.
185→
186→Note that due to renaming and relocation of the `ReporterProviderV2` interface the name of the service provider in the custom reporter needs to be changed from `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ReporterProvider` to `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterProviderV2`.
187→
188→The biggest change in the `ReporterV2` is the replacement of the `LintError` class with `KtlintCliError` class. The latter class now contains a status field which more clearly explains the difference between a lint error which can be autocorrected versus a lint error that actually has been autocorrected.
189→
190→#### Custom rules provided by API Consumer
191→
192→API Consumers provide a set of rules directly to the Ktlint Rule Engine. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule`.
193→
194→The type of the rule id's has been changed from type `String` to `RuleId`. A `RuleId` has a value that must adhere the convention "`rule-set-id`:`rule-id`". Rule set id `standard` is reserved for rules which are maintained by the KtLint project. Custom rules created by the API Consumer should use a prefix other than `standard` to clearly mark the origin of rules which are not maintained by the KtLint project.
195→
196→Also, the field `About` has been added. This field specifies the name of the maintainer, and the repository url and issue tracker url of the rule.
197→
198→When wrapping a rule from the ktlint project and modifying its behavior, please change the `ruleId` and `about` fields in the wrapped rule, so that it is clear to users whenever they use the original rule provided by KtLint versus a modified version which is not maintained by the KtLint project.
199→
200→The typealias `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig`. The interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties` has been removed. Instead, the Rule property `usesEditorConfigProperties` needs to be set. As a result of those changes, the `beforeFirstNode` function in each rule has to changed to something like below:
201→
202→```kotlin
203→public class SomeRule : Rule(
204→  ruleId = RuleId("some-rule-set:some-rule"),
205→  usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY),
206→) {
207→  private lateinit var myEditorConfigProperty: MyEditorConfigProperty
208→
209→  override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
210→    myEditorConfigProperty = editorConfig[MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY]
211→  }
212→  
213→  ...
214→}
215→```
216→
217→Fields `loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded` and `runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled` have been removed from class `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule` and are replaced with a single field `mode`. The `mode` either contains value `REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED` or `ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED`.
218→
219→Like before, the API Consumer can still offer a mix of rules originating from `ktlint-ruleset-standard` as well as custom rules.
220→
221→#### `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` default value
222→
223→Previously the default value for `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` was set to `-1` in ktlint unless the property was defined explicitly in the `.editorconfig` or when `ktlint_code_style` was set to Android. As a result of that rules have to check that max_line_length contains a positive value before checking that the actual line length is exceeding the maximum. Now the value `Int.MAX_VALUE` (use constant `MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF` to refer to that value) is used instead. 
224→
225→### Added
226→
227→* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-first-line-in-class-body` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rule disallows a class to start with a blank line. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
228→* Add new experimental rule `if-else-bracing` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rules enforces consistent usage of braces in all branches of a singe if or if-else-if statement. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
229→* Add new experimental rule `no-consecutive-comments` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rule disallows consecutive comments except EOL comments. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
230→* Add new experimental rule `try-catch-finally-spacing` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rule enforces consistent spacing in try-catch, try-finally and try-catch-finally statement. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
231→* Wrap the type or value of a function or class parameter in case the maximum line length is exceeded `parameter-wrapping` ([#1846](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1846))
232→* Wrap the type or value of a property in case the maximum line length is exceeded `property-wrapping` ([#1846](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1846))
233→
234→### Removed
235→
236→* Remove support of the `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules`. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
237→
238→### Fixed
239→
240→* An enumeration class having a primary constructor and in which the list of enum entries is followed by a semicolon then do not remove the semicolon in case it is followed by code element `no-semi` ([#1733](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1733))
241→* Do not add the (first line of the) body expression on the same line as the function signature in case the max line length would be exceeded. `function-signature`. 
242→* Do not add the first line of a multiline body expression on the same line as the function signature in case function body expression wrapping property is set to `multiline`. `function-signature`. 
243→* Disable the `standard:filename` rule whenever Ktlint CLI is run with option `--stdin` ([#1742](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1742))
244→* The parameters of a function literal containing a multiline parameter list are aligned with first parameter whenever the first parameter is on the same line as the start of that function literal (not allowed in `ktlint_official` code style) `indent` ([#1756](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1756))
245→* Do not throw exception when enum class does not contain entries `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` ([#1711](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1711))
246→* Fix continuation indent for a dot qualified array access expression in `ktlint_official` code style only `indent` ([#1540](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1540))
247→* When generating the `.editorconfig` use value `off` for the `max_line_length` property instead of value `-1` to denote that lines are not restricted to a maximum length ([#1824](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1824))
248→* Do not report an "unnecessary semicolon" after adding a trailing comma to an enum class containing a code element after the last enum entry `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` ([#1786](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1786))
249→* A newline before a function return type should not be removed in case that leads to exceeding the maximum line length `function-return-type-spacing` ([#1764](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1764))

 62→@Command(
 63→    headerHeading =
 64→    """
 65→An anti-bikeshedding Kotlin linter with built-in formatter.
 66→(https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint).
 67→
 68→Usage:
 69→  ktlint <flags> [patterns]
 70→  java -jar ktlint.jar <flags> [patterns]
 71→
 72→Examples:
 73→  # Check the style of all Kotlin files (ending with '.kt' or '.kts') inside the current dir (recursively).
 74→  #
 75→  # Hidden folders will be skipped.
 76→  ktlint
 77→
 78→  # Check only certain locations starting from the current directory.
 79→  #
 80→  # Prepend ! to negate the pattern, KtLint uses .gitignore pattern style syntax.
 81→  # Globs are applied starting from the last one.
 82→  #
 83→  # Hidden folders will be skipped.
 84→  # Check all '.kt' files in 'src/' directory, but ignore files ending with 'Test.kt':
 85→  ktlint "src/**/*.kt" "!src/**/*Test.kt"
 86→  # Check all '.kt' files in 'src/' directory, but ignore 'generated' directory and its subdirectories:
 87→  ktlint "src/**/*.kt" "!src/**/generated/**"
 88→
 89→  # Auto-correct style violations.
 90→  ktlint -F "src/**/*.kt"
 91→
 92→  # Using custom reporter jar and overriding report location
 93→  ktlint --reporter=csv,artifact=/path/to/reporter/csv.jar,output=my-custom-report.csv
 94→Flags:
 95→""",
 96→    synopsisHeading = "",
 97→    customSynopsis = [""],
 98→    sortOptions = false,
 99→    mixinStandardHelpOptions = true,
100→    versionProvider = KtlintVersionProvider::class,
101→)
