237→    private val tripped = AtomicBoolean()
238→    private val fileNumber = AtomicInteger()
239→    private val errorNumber = AtomicInteger()
240→
241→    internal var debug: Boolean = false
242→        get() = Level.DEBUG.isGreaterOrEqual(minLogLevel)
243→        private set
244→
245→    private fun disabledRulesEditorConfigOverrides() =
246→        disabledRules
247→            .split(",")
248→            .filter { it.isNotBlank() }
249→            .map {
250→                // For backwards compatibility, prefix the rule id with the standard rule set id when missing
251→                RuleId.prefixWithStandardRuleSetIdWhenMissing(it)
252→            }.map { RuleId(it).createRuleExecutionEditorConfigProperty() to RuleExecution.disabled }
253→            .toTypedArray()
254→
255→    fun run() {
256→        val editorConfigOverride = EditorConfigOverride
257→            .EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE
258→            .applyIf(experimental) {
259→                logger.debug { "Add editor config override to allow the experimental rule set" }
260→                plus(EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY to RuleExecution.enabled)
261→            }.applyIf(disabledRules.isNotBlank()) {
262→                logger.debug { "Add editor config override to disable rules: '$disabledRules'" }
263→                plus(*disabledRulesEditorConfigOverrides())
264→            }.applyIf(android) {
265→                logger.debug { "Add editor config override to set code style to 'android'" }
266→                plus(CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY to CodeStyleValue.android)
267→            }.applyIf(stdin) {
268→                logger.debug {
269→                    "Add editor config override to disable 'filename' rule which can not be used in combination with reading from <stdin>"
270→                }
271→                plus(FILENAME_RULE_ID.createRuleExecutionEditorConfigProperty() to RuleExecution.disabled)
272→            }
273→
274→        if (android) {
275→            logger.error {
276→                "Option '--android' / '-a' is deprecated and replaced with option '--code-style=android_studio'. Setting '.editorconfig' " +
277→                    "property 'ktlint_code_style=android_studio' might be a better idea for a project that is always to formatted with " +
278→                    "this code style."
279→            }
280→        }
281→        assertStdinAndPatternsFromStdinOptionsMutuallyExclusive()
282→        patterns = patterns.replaceWithPatternsFromStdinOrDefaultPatternsWhenEmpty()
283→
284→        val start = System.currentTimeMillis()
285→
286→        val ruleProviders = ruleProviders()
287→        val ktLintRuleEngine = KtLintRuleEngine(
288→            ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
289→            editorConfigDefaults = editorConfigDefaults(ruleProviders),
290→            editorConfigOverride = editorConfigOverride,
291→            isInvokedFromCli = true,
292→        )
293→
294→        val baseline = if (stdin || baselinePath.isBlank()) {
295→            Baseline(status = Baseline.Status.DISABLED)
296→        } else {
297→            loadBaseline(baselinePath)
298→        }
299→
300→        val aggregatedReporter = ReporterAggregator(
301→            baseline,
302→            reporterConfigurations,
303→            color,
304→            colorName,
305→            stdin,
306→            format,
307→            relative,
308→        ).aggregatedReporter()
309→
310→        aggregatedReporter.beforeAll()
311→        if (stdin) {
312→            lintStdin(
313→                ktLintRuleEngine,
314→                aggregatedReporter,
315→            )
316→        } else {
317→            lintFiles(
318→                ktLintRuleEngine,
319→                baseline.lintErrorsPerFile,
320→                aggregatedReporter,
321→            )
322→        }
323→        aggregatedReporter.afterAll()
324→
325→        logger.debug {
326→            "Finished processing in ${System.currentTimeMillis() - start}ms / $fileNumber file(s) scanned / $errorNumber error(s) found"
327→        }
328→        if (fileNumber.get() == 0) {
329→            // Do not return an error as this would implicate that in a multi-module project, each module has to contain
330→            // at least one kotlin file.
331→            logger.warn { "No files matched $patterns" }
332→        }
333→        if (tripped.get()) {
334→            exitKtLintProcess(1)
335→        } else {
336→            exitKtLintProcess(0)
337→        }
338→    }
339→
340→    private fun editorConfigDefaults(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>): EditorConfigDefaults {
341→        val fullyExpandedEditorConfigPath =
342→            editorConfigPath
343→                ?.expandTildeToFullPath()
344→                ?.let { path -> Paths.get(path) }
345→        return EditorConfigDefaults.load(fullyExpandedEditorConfigPath, ruleProviders.propertyTypes())
346→    }
347→
348→    private fun List<String>.replaceWithPatternsFromStdinOrDefaultPatternsWhenEmpty(): List<String> {
349→        val localStdinDelimiter: String? = stdinDelimiter
350→        return when {
351→            localStdinDelimiter != null -> {
352→                val stdinPatterns: Set<String> = readPatternsFromStdin(localStdinDelimiter.ifEmpty { "\u0000" })
353→                if (isNotEmpty() && stdinPatterns.isNotEmpty()) {
354→                    logger.warn {
355→                        "Patterns specified at command line ($this) and patterns from 'stdin' due to flag '--patterns-from-stdin' " +
356→                            "($stdinPatterns) are merged"
357→                    }
358→                }
359→                // Note: it is okay in case both the original patterns and the patterns from stdin are empty
360→                this.plus(stdinPatterns)
361→            }
362→            this.isEmpty() -> {
363→                logger.info { "Enable default patterns $DEFAULT_PATTERNS" }
364→                DEFAULT_PATTERNS
365→            }
366→            else -> {
367→                // Keep original patterns
368→                this
369→            }
370→        }
371→    }

373→    // Do not convert to "val" as the function depends on PicoCli options which are not fully instantiated until the "run" method is started
374→    internal fun ruleProviders(): Set<RuleProvider> = loadRuleProviders(rulesetJarPaths.toFilesURIList())

376→    // Do not convert to "val" as the function depends on PicoCli options which are not fully instantiated until the "run" method is started
377→    internal fun configureLogger() {
378→        logger = KotlinLogging
379→            .logger {}
380→            .setDefaultLoggerModifier { logger ->
381→                (logger.underlyingLogger as Logger).level = minLogLevel
382→            }
383→            .initKtLintKLogger()
384→    }
385→
386→    private fun assertStdinAndPatternsFromStdinOptionsMutuallyExclusive() {
387→        if (stdin && stdinDelimiter != null) {
388→            throw ParameterException(
389→                commandSpec.commandLine(),
390→                "Options --stdin and --patterns-from-stdin are mutually exclusive",
391→            )
392→        }
393→    }
394→
395→    private fun lintFiles(
396→        ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
397→        lintErrorsPerFile: Map<String, List<KtlintCliError>>,
398→        reporter: ReporterV2,
399→    ) {
400→        FileSystems
401→            .getDefault()
402→            .fileSequence(patterns)
403→            .map { it.toFile() }
404→            .takeWhile { errorNumber.get() < limit }
405→            .map { file ->
406→                val fileName = file.toPath().absolutePathString()
407→                Callable {
408→                    fileName to process(
409→                        ktLintRuleEngine = ktLintRuleEngine,
410→                        code = Code.fromFile(file),
411→                        baselineLintErrors = lintErrorsPerFile.getOrDefault(fileName, emptyList()),
412→                    )
413→                }
414→            }.parallel({ (fileName, errList) -> report(Paths.get(fileName).relativeRoute, errList, reporter) })
415→    }
416→
417→    private fun lintStdin(
418→        ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
419→        reporter: ReporterV2,
420→    ) {
421→        report(
422→            KtLintRuleEngine.STDIN_FILE,
423→            process(
424→                ktLintRuleEngine = ktLintRuleEngine,
425→                code = Code.fromStdin(),
426→                baselineLintErrors = emptyList(),
427→            ),
428→            reporter,
429→        )
430→    }
431→
432→    private fun report(
433→        fileName: String,
434→        ktlintCliErrors: List<KtlintCliError>,
435→        reporter: ReporterV2,
436→    ) {
437→        fileNumber.incrementAndGet()
438→        val errListLimit = minOf(ktlintCliErrors.size, maxOf(limit - errorNumber.get(), 0))
439→        errorNumber.addAndGet(errListLimit)
440→
441→        val relativeRoute = Paths.get(fileName).relativeRoute
442→        reporter.before(relativeRoute)
443→        ktlintCliErrors
444→            .take(errListLimit)
445→            .forEach { reporter.onLintError(relativeRoute, it) }
446→        reporter.after(relativeRoute)
447→    }
448→
449→    private fun process(
450→        ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
451→        code: Code,
452→        baselineLintErrors: List<KtlintCliError>,
453→    ): List<KtlintCliError> {
454→        if (code.fileName != null) {
455→            logger.trace { "Checking ${File(code.fileName!!).location(relative)}" }
456→        }
457→        val result = ArrayList<KtlintCliError>()
458→        if (format) {
459→            val formattedFileContent = try {
460→                ktLintRuleEngine.format(code) { lintError, corrected ->
461→                    val ktlintCliError = KtlintCliError(
462→                        line = lintError.line,
463→                        col = lintError.col,
464→                        ruleId = lintError.ruleId,
465→                        detail = lintError
466→                            .detail
467→                            .applyIf(corrected) { "$this (cannot be auto-corrected)" },
468→                        status = if (corrected) {
469→                            FORMAT_IS_AUTOCORRECTED
470→                        } else {
471→                            LINT_CAN_NOT_BE_AUTOCORRECTED
472→                        },
473→                    )
474→                    if (baselineLintErrors.doesNotContain(ktlintCliError)) {
475→                        result.add(ktlintCliError)
476→                        if (!corrected) {
477→                            tripped.set(true)
478→                        }
479→                    }
480→                }
481→            } catch (e: Exception) {
482→                result.add(e.toKtlintCliError(code))
483→                tripped.set(true)
484→                code.content // making sure `cat file | ktlint --stdin > file` is (relatively) safe
485→            }
486→            when {
487→                code.isStdIn -> print(formattedFileContent)
488→                code.content != formattedFileContent ->
489→                    code
490→                        .filePath
491→                        ?.toFile()
492→                        ?.writeText(formattedFileContent, charset("UTF-8"))
493→            }
494→        } else {
495→            try {
496→                ktLintRuleEngine.lint(code) { lintError ->
497→                    val ktlintCliError = KtlintCliError(
498→                        line = lintError.line,
499→                        col = lintError.col,
500→                        ruleId = lintError.ruleId,
501→                        detail = lintError.detail,
502→                        status = if (lintError.canBeAutoCorrected) {
503→                            LINT_CAN_BE_AUTOCORRECTED
504→                        } else {
505→                            LINT_CAN_NOT_BE_AUTOCORRECTED
506→                        },
507→                    )
508→                    if (baselineLintErrors.doesNotContain(ktlintCliError)) {
509→                        result.add(ktlintCliError)
510→                        tripped.set(true)
511→                    }
512→                }
513→            } catch (e: Exception) {
514→                result.add(e.toKtlintCliError(code))
515→                tripped.set(true)
516→            }
517→        }
518→        return result
519→    }
520→
521→    private fun Exception.toKtlintCliError(code: Code): KtlintCliError =
522→        this.let { e ->
523→            when (e) {
524→                is KtLintParseException ->
525→                    KtlintCliError(
526→                        line = e.line,
527→                        col = e.col,
528→                        ruleId = "",
529→                        detail = "Not a valid Kotlin file (${e.message?.lowercase(Locale.getDefault())})",
530→                        status = KOTLIN_PARSE_EXCEPTION,
531→                    )
532→                is KtLintRuleException -> {
533→                    val codeSource = if (code.isStdIn) {
534→                        "code"
535→                    } else {
536→                        "file '${code.fileName}'"
537→                    }
538→                    logger.debug("Internal Error (${e.ruleId}) in $codeSource at position '${e.line}:${e.col}", e)
539→                    KtlintCliError(
540→                        line = e.line,
541→                        col = e.col,
542→                        ruleId = "",
543→                        detail = "Internal Error (rule '${e.ruleId}') in $codeSource at position '${e.line}:${e.col}. " +
544→                            "Please create a ticket at https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues " +
545→                            "and provide the source code that triggered an error.\n" +
546→                            e.stackTraceToString(),
547→                        status = KTLINT_RULE_ENGINE_EXCEPTION,
548→                    )
549→                }
550→                else -> throw e
551→            }
552→        }
553→
554→    private fun readPatternsFromStdin(delimiter: String): Set<String> {
555→        require(delimiter.isNotEmpty())
556→
557→        return String(System.`in`.readBytes())
558→            .split(delimiter)
559→            .let { patterns: List<String> ->
560→                patterns.filterTo(LinkedHashSet(patterns.size), String::isNotEmpty)
561→            }
562→    }
563→
564→    /**
565→     * Executes "Callable"s in parallel (lazily).
566→     * The results are gathered one-by-one (by `cb(<callable result>)`) in the order of corresponding "Callable"s
567→     * in the "Sequence" (think `seq.toList().map { executorService.submit(it) }.forEach { cb(it.get()) }` but without
568→     * buffering an entire sequence).
569→     *
570→     * Once kotlinx-coroutines are out of "experimental" stage everything below can be replaced with
571→     * ```
572→     * suspend fun <T> Sequence<Callable<T>>.parallel(...) {
573→     *     val ctx = newFixedThreadPoolContext(numberOfThreads, "Sequence<Callable<T>>.parallel")
574→     *     ctx.use {
575→     *         val channel = produce(ctx, numberOfThreads) {
576→     *             for (task in this@parallel) {
577→     *                 send(async(ctx) { task.call() })
578→     *             }
579→     *         }
580→     *         for (res in channel) {
581→     *             cb(res.await())
582→     *         }
583→     *     }
584→     * }
585→     * ```
586→     */
587→    private fun <T> Sequence<Callable<T>>.parallel(
588→        cb: (T) -> Unit,
589→        numberOfThreads: Int = Runtime.getRuntime().availableProcessors(),
590→    ) {
591→        val pill = object : Future<T> {
592→            override fun isDone(): Boolean {
593→                throw UnsupportedOperationException()
594→            }
595→
596→            override fun get(
597→                timeout: Long,
598→                unit: TimeUnit,
599→            ): T {
600→                throw UnsupportedOperationException()
601→            }
602→
603→            override fun get(): T {
604→                throw UnsupportedOperationException()
605→            }
606→
607→            override fun cancel(mayInterruptIfRunning: Boolean): Boolean {
608→                throw UnsupportedOperationException()
609→            }
610→
611→            override fun isCancelled(): Boolean {
612→                throw UnsupportedOperationException()
613→            }
614→        }
615→        val q = ArrayBlockingQueue<Future<T>>(numberOfThreads)
616→        val producer = thread(start = true) {
617→            val executorService = Executors.newCachedThreadPool()
618→            try {
619→                for (task in this) {
620→                    q.put(executorService.submit(task))
621→                }
622→                q.put(pill)
623→            } catch (e: InterruptedException) {
624→                // we've been asked to stop consuming sequence
625→            } finally {
626→                executorService.shutdown()
627→            }
628→        }
629→        try {
630→            while (true) {
631→                val result = q.take()
632→                if (result != pill) cb(result.get()) else break
633→            }
634→        } finally {
635→            producer.interrupt() // in case q.take()/result.get() throws
636→            producer.join()
637→        }
638→    }
639→}

641→private class CodeStyleValueConverter : CommandLine.ITypeConverter<CodeStyleValue> {
642→    @Throws(Exception::class)
643→    override fun convert(value: String?): CodeStyleValue =
644→        when (value?.lowercase()?.replace("-", "_")) {
645→            null -> CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
646→            "ktlint_official" -> CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official
647→            "android_studio" -> CodeStyleValue.android_studio
648→            "intellij_idea" -> CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea
649→            else -> throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid code style value")
650→        }
651→}
652→
653→private class LogLevelConverter : CommandLine.ITypeConverter<Level> {
654→    @Throws(Exception::class)
655→    override fun convert(value: String?): Level =
656→        when (value?.uppercase()) {
657→            "TRACE" -> Level.TRACE
658→            "DEBUG" -> Level.DEBUG
659→            "INFO" -> Level.INFO
660→            "WARN" -> Level.WARN
661→            "ERROR" -> Level.ERROR
662→            "NONE" -> Level.OFF
663→            else -> throw IllegalStateException("Invalid log level '$value'")
664→        }
665→}

667→// Do not convert to "val" as the function depends on PicoCli options which are not fully instantiated until the "run" method is started
668→internal fun List<String>.toFilesURIList() =
669→    map {
670→        val jarFile = File(it.expandTildeToFullPath())
671→        if (!jarFile.exists()) {
672→            logger.error { "File '$it' does not exist" }
673→            exitKtLintProcess(1)
674→        }
675→        jarFile.toURI().toURL()
676→    }

678→/**
679→ * Gets the relative route of the path. Also adjusts the slashes for uniformity between file systems.
680→ */
681→internal val Path.relativeRoute: String
682→    get() {
683→        val rootPath = Paths.get("").toAbsolutePath()
684→        return this
685→            .relativeToOrSelf(rootPath)
686→            .pathString
687→            .replace(File.separatorChar, '/')
688→    }

690→/**
691→ * Wrapper around exitProcess which ensure that a proper log line is written which can be used in unit tests for
692→ * validating the result of the test.
693→ */
694→internal fun exitKtLintProcess(status: Int): Nothing {
695→    logger.debug { "Exit ktlint with exit code: $status" }
696→    exitProcess(status)
697→}
